```{r combinedTableLoc3_basin_specific_models, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Response variable display labels with HTML superscripts
response_labels <- c(
  meanDeltaMass_mgcm2y = "Mass (mg cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanDeltaVolume      = "Volume (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanDeltaDensity     = "Density (mg cm<sup>-3</sup>cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanAccretion        = "Accretion (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanGrazing          = "Grazing (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanMacroboring      = "Macroboring (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)"
)

# Define model term order for the full coefficient table
term_order <- c(
  "(Intercept)",
  "comm_bioerosion_mean",
  "dist_city_km",
  "meanDepth"
)

# Model term display labels
term_labels <- c(
  `(Intercept)`        = "Intercept",
  comm_bioerosion_mean = "Parrotfish pressure",
  dist_city_km         = "Urban influence",
  meanDepth            = "Depth"
)

# Build model data
loc3_model_long <- combinedTableLoc3 %>%
  select(
    basin, loc1, loc2, loc3,
    comm_bioerosion_mean,
    dist_city_km,
    meanDepth,
    all_of(response_vars)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(response_vars),
    names_to = "response",
    values_to = "mean_response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    response = factor(response, levels = response_vars)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    basin %in% c("atlantic", "pacific"),
    !is.na(mean_response),
    !is.na(comm_bioerosion_mean)
  )

# Function to apply basin-specific NA filtering
filter_model_data <- function(dat, loc3ModelBasin) {
  if (loc3ModelBasin == "atlantic") {
    dat %>%
      filter(!is.na(comm_bioerosion_mean))
  } else if (loc3ModelBasin == "pacific") {
    dat %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(comm_bioerosion_mean),
        !is.na(dist_city_km),
        !is.na(meanDepth)
      )
  } else {
    stop("Unexpected basin name: ", loc3ModelBasin)
  }
}

# Function to fit basin-specific loc3-level models
# Atlantic: response ~ parrotfish pressure only
# Pacific:  response ~ parrotfish pressure + urban influence + depth
fit_loc3_lm <- function(dat, loc3ModelBasin) {
  if (loc3ModelBasin == "atlantic") {
    lm(
      mean_response ~ comm_bioerosion_mean,
      data = dat
    )
  } else if (loc3ModelBasin == "pacific") {
    lm(
      mean_response ~ comm_bioerosion_mean + dist_city_km + meanDepth,
      data = dat
    )
  } else {
    stop("Unexpected basin name: ", loc3ModelBasin)
  }
}

# Function to extract model coefficients and model fit statistics
extract_lm_effects <- function(dat, loc3ModelBasin) {
  model_summary <- summary(fit_loc3_lm(dat, loc3ModelBasin))

  as.data.frame(model_summary$coefficients) %>%
    rownames_to_column("term") %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    mutate(
      r_squared = model_summary$r.squared,
      adj_r_squared = model_summary$adj.r.squared
    )
}

# Fit models and create full coefficient table
full_model_effects <- loc3_model_long %>%
  group_by(basin, response) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model_data = map2(data, basin, filter_model_data),
    n_loc3 = map_int(model_data, nrow),
    n_loc2 = map_int(model_data, ~ n_distinct(.x$loc2)),
    n_loc1 = map_int(model_data, ~ n_distinct(.x$loc1)),
    coef_table = map2(model_data, basin, extract_lm_effects)
  ) %>%
  select(
    basin, response,
    n_loc3, n_loc2, n_loc1,
    coef_table
  ) %>%
  unnest(coef_table) %>%
  rename(
    estimate  = Estimate,
    std.error = `Std. Error`,
    statistic = `t value`,
    p.value   = `Pr(>|t|)`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    response = factor(response, levels = response_vars),
    term = factor(term, levels = term_order)
  ) %>%
  select(
    basin, response,
    n_loc3, n_loc2, n_loc1,
    r_squared, adj_r_squared,
    term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value
  ) %>%
  arrange(response, basin, term) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create display table with formatted labels and capitalization
full_model_effects_display <- full_model_effects %>%
  mutate(
    Response = response_labels[as.character(response)],
    Basin = str_to_title(basin),
    Term = term_labels[as.character(term)]
  ) %>%
  select(
    Response, Basin,
    n_loc3, n_loc2, n_loc1,
    r_squared, adj_r_squared,
    Term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value
  ) %>%
  rename(
    `N loc3` = n_loc3,
    `N loc2` = n_loc2,
    `N loc1` = n_loc1,
    `R-squared` = r_squared,
    `Adjusted R-squared` = adj_r_squared,
    Estimate = estimate,
    `Std. Error` = std.error,
    `t value` = statistic,
    `p value` = p.value
  )

# Identify significant non-intercept term rows
sig_rows <- which(
  full_model_effects$p.value < 0.05 &
    as.character(full_model_effects$term) != "(Intercept)"
)

# Table of full model effects
full_model_effects_display %>%
  kbl(
    digits = 4,
    escape = FALSE,
    caption = "Full loc3-level model coefficient table using combinedTableLoc3. Atlantic models include parrotfish pressure only. Pacific models include parrotfish pressure, urban influence, and depth."
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE
  ) %>%
  row_spec(
    sig_rows,
    bold = TRUE
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(
    columns = 1,
    valign = "top",
    latex_hline = "major"
  ) %>%
  scroll_box(height = "1500px", width = "100%")
```


```{r loc3_parrotfish_pressure_model_prediction_plots, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)

# Responses to plot, in the requested order
response_lookup <- tibble(
  response_var = c("meanGrazing", "meanAccretion", "meanDeltaVolume"),
  response_label = c("Grazing", "Accretion", "Volume")
)

y_axis_labels <- list(
  meanGrazing = expression("Grazing (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")"),
  meanAccretion = expression("Accretion (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")"),
  meanDeltaVolume = expression("Volume (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")")
)

# Consistent basin colors
basin_colors <- c(
  Atlantic = "#D55E00",
  Pacific = "#0072B2"
)

basin_shapes <- c(
  Atlantic = 16,
  Pacific = 17
)

# Build long-format plotting/modeling dataset
loc3_parrotfish_dat <- combinedTableLoc3 %>%
  select(
    basin, loc1, loc2, loc3,
    comm_bioerosion_mean,
    dist_city_km,
    meanDepth,
    all_of(response_lookup$response_var)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(response_lookup$response_var),
    names_to = "response_var",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  left_join(response_lookup, by = "response_var") %>%
  mutate(
    basin = str_to_title(basin)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    basin %in% c("Atlantic", "Pacific"),
    !is.na(value),
    !is.na(comm_bioerosion_mean)
  )

# Fit basin-specific models
# Atlantic: response ~ parrotfish pressure
# Pacific: response ~ parrotfish pressure + urban influence + depth
model_fits <- loc3_parrotfish_dat %>%
  group_by(response_var, response_label, basin) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    fit = map2(
      data,
      basin,
      ~ if (.y == "Atlantic") {
        lm(value ~ comm_bioerosion_mean, data = .x)
      } else {
        lm(value ~ comm_bioerosion_mean + dist_city_km + meanDepth, data = .x)
      }
    )
  )

# Extract parrotfish-pressure p values
parrotfish_pvals <- model_fits %>%
  mutate(tidy_fit = map(fit, tidy)) %>%
  select(response_var, response_label, basin, tidy_fit) %>%
  unnest(tidy_fit) %>%
  filter(term == "comm_bioerosion_mean") %>%
  mutate(
    significant = p.value < 0.05,
    p_label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ paste0(basin, ": p < 0.001"),
      TRUE ~ paste0(basin, ": p = ", formatC(p.value, format = "f", digits = 3))
    )
  )

# Build prediction data
# For Pacific, urban influence and depth are held constant at Pacific means
prediction_dat <- model_fits %>%
  mutate(
    pred_dat = map2(
      data,
      basin,
      ~ {
        x_seq <- seq(
          min(.x$comm_bioerosion_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
          max(.x$comm_bioerosion_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
          length.out = 200
        )
        
        if (.y == "Atlantic") {
          tibble(
            comm_bioerosion_mean = x_seq
          )
        } else {
          tibble(
            comm_bioerosion_mean = x_seq,
            dist_city_km = mean(.x$dist_city_km, na.rm = TRUE),
            meanDepth = mean(.x$meanDepth, na.rm = TRUE)
          )
        }
      }
    ),
    predictions = map2(
      fit,
      pred_dat,
      ~ bind_cols(
        .y,
        as_tibble(
          predict(
            .x,
            newdata = .y,
            interval = "confidence",
            level = 0.95
          )
        )
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  select(response_var, response_label, basin, predictions) %>%
  unnest(predictions) %>%
  left_join(
    parrotfish_pvals %>%
      select(response_var, basin, significant),
    by = c("response_var", "basin")
  ) %>%
  filter(significant)

# Fixed p-value label positions in lower right so they do not overlap
label_dat <- parrotfish_pvals %>%
  left_join(
    loc3_parrotfish_dat %>%
      group_by(response_var, response_label) %>%
      summarize(
        x_min = min(comm_bioerosion_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
        x_max = max(comm_bioerosion_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("response_var", "response_label")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    x = x_max - 0.02 * (x_max - x_min),
    basin = factor(basin, levels = c("Atlantic", "Pacific"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(response_var, basin) %>%
  group_by(response_var, response_label) %>%
  mutate(
    y = case_when(
      row_number() == 1 ~ -75,
      row_number() == 2 ~ -90,
      TRUE ~ -90
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plotting function
plot_response <- function(response_var_name) {
  
  response_title <- response_lookup %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name) %>%
    pull(response_label)
  
  y_lab <- y_axis_labels[[response_var_name]]
  
  point_dat <- loc3_parrotfish_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  line_dat <- prediction_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  label_subdat <- label_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = point_dat,
      aes(
        x = comm_bioerosion_mean,
        y = value,
        color = basin,
        shape = basin
      ),
      size = 2.6,
      alpha = 0.80
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = line_dat,
      aes(
        x = comm_bioerosion_mean,
        ymin = lwr,
        ymax = upr,
        fill = basin,
        group = basin
      ),
      alpha = 0.18,
      color = NA
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = line_dat,
      aes(
        x = comm_bioerosion_mean,
        y = fit,
        color = basin,
        group = basin
      ),
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_label(
      data = label_subdat,
      aes(
        x = x,
        y = y,
        label = p_label,
        color = basin
      ),
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 0,
      size = 3,
      label.size = NA,
      fill = "white",
      alpha = 0.85,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = basin_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = basin_colors) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = basin_shapes) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-100, 50)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-100, 50, by = 25)) +
    labs(
      x = "Parrotfish pressure",
      y = y_lab,
      title = response_title,
      color = "Basin",
      fill = "Basin",
      shape = "Basin"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

# Build plots in requested order
grazing_plot <- plot_response("meanGrazing")
accretion_plot <- plot_response("meanAccretion")
volume_plot <- plot_response("meanDeltaVolume")

# Arrange panels
grazing_plot + accretion_plot + volume_plot +
  plot_layout(ncol = 3, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```




```{r loc3_urban_influence_model_prediction_plots, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)

# Responses to plot
response_lookup <- tibble(
  response_var = c("meanGrazing", "meanAccretion", "meanDeltaVolume", "meanMacroboring"),
  response_label = c("Grazing", "Accretion", "Volume", "Macroboring")
)

y_axis_labels <- list(
  meanGrazing = expression("Grazing (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")"),
  meanAccretion = expression("Accretion (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")"),
  meanDeltaVolume = expression("Volume (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")"),
  meanMacroboring = expression("Macroboring (mm"^3*" cm"^-2*" yr"^-1*")")
)

# Pacific-only dataset
loc3_urban_dat <- combinedTableLoc3 %>%
  select(
    basin, loc1, loc2, loc3,
    comm_bioerosion_mean,
    dist_city_km,
    meanDepth,
    all_of(response_lookup$response_var)
  ) %>%
  filter(basin == "pacific") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(response_lookup$response_var),
    names_to = "response_var",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  left_join(response_lookup, by = "response_var") %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(value),
    !is.na(dist_city_km),
    !is.na(comm_bioerosion_mean),
    !is.na(meanDepth)
  )

# Fit Pacific models
# response ~ parrotfish pressure + urban influence + depth
model_fits <- loc3_urban_dat %>%
  group_by(response_var, response_label) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    fit = map(
      data,
      ~ lm(value ~ comm_bioerosion_mean + dist_city_km + meanDepth, data = .x)
    )
  )

# Extract urban-influence p values
urban_pvals <- model_fits %>%
  mutate(tidy_fit = map(fit, tidy)) %>%
  select(response_var, response_label, tidy_fit) %>%
  unnest(tidy_fit) %>%
  filter(term == "dist_city_km") %>%
  mutate(
    significant = p.value < 0.05,
    p_label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "p < 0.001",
      TRUE ~ paste0("p = ", formatC(p.value, format = "f", digits = 3))
    )
  )

# Build prediction data
# Hold parrotfish pressure and depth constant at Pacific means
prediction_dat <- model_fits %>%
  mutate(
    pred_dat = map(
      data,
      ~ tibble(
        dist_city_km = seq(
          min(.x$dist_city_km, na.rm = TRUE),
          max(.x$dist_city_km, na.rm = TRUE),
          length.out = 200
        ),
        comm_bioerosion_mean = mean(.x$comm_bioerosion_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
        meanDepth = mean(.x$meanDepth, na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    ),
    predictions = map2(
      fit,
      pred_dat,
      ~ bind_cols(
        .y,
        as_tibble(
          predict(
            .x,
            newdata = .y,
            interval = "confidence",
            level = 0.95
          )
        )
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  select(response_var, response_label, predictions) %>%
  unnest(predictions)

# Lower-right p-value positions for each plot
label_dat <- loc3_urban_dat %>%
  group_by(response_var, response_label) %>%
  summarize(
    x_min = min(dist_city_km, na.rm = TRUE),
    x_max = max(dist_city_km, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(urban_pvals, by = c("response_var", "response_label")) %>%
  mutate(
    x = x_max - 0.02 * (x_max - x_min),
    y = y_min + 0.06 * (y_max - y_min)
  )

# Plotting function
plot_response <- function(response_var_name) {
  
  response_title <- response_lookup %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name) %>%
    pull(response_label)
  
  y_lab <- y_axis_labels[[response_var_name]]
  
  point_dat <- loc3_urban_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  line_dat <- prediction_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  label_subdat <- label_dat %>%
    filter(response_var == response_var_name)
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = point_dat,
      aes(
        x = dist_city_km,
        y = value
      ),
      size = 2.6,
      alpha = 0.80
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = line_dat,
      aes(
        x = dist_city_km,
        ymin = lwr,
        ymax = upr
      ),
      alpha = 0.18
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = line_dat,
      aes(
        x = dist_city_km,
        y = fit
      ),
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_label(
      data = label_subdat,
      aes(
        x = x,
        y = y,
        label = p_label
      ),
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 0,
      size = 3,
      label.size = NA,
      fill = "white",
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Distance to nearest city (km)",
      y = y_lab,
      title = response_title
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA)
    )
}

# Build plots in preferred order
grazing_plot <- plot_response("meanGrazing")
accretion_plot <- plot_response("meanAccretion")
volume_plot <- plot_response("meanDeltaVolume")
macroboring_plot <- plot_response("meanMacroboring")

# Arrange panels
(grazing_plot + accretion_plot) /
(volume_plot + macroboring_plot)
```


















# Run linear models for Loc2 site-level analysis 
Linear models were used in each basin to investigate the impact of aragonite saturation state on BAR responses. Models were run on loc2-averaged values, with depth and chlorophyll included as covariates. After depth and chlorophyll were accounted for, omega relationships were evaluated separately for each response variable and basin.

Note sites like St. Thomas and Wake with no density are not included in the analysis only of those parameters, as listed with NAs.

This is what ChatGPT told me about removing depth (don't use):
"Depth was not a predictor of interest, it did not improve interpretation, and including it costs degrees of freedom in a small loc2-level dataset."


## Are loc2 environmental predictors collinear?
```{r loc2_environmental_predictor_collinearity_table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Build loc2 predictor dataset
loc2_predictor_dat <- combinedTableLoc2 %>%
  select(
    basin, loc1, loc2,
    meanChlorophyll,
    meanTemperature,
    meanDepth,
    mean_AragSatState
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Chlorophyll = meanChlorophyll,
    Temperature = meanTemperature,
    Depth = meanDepth,
    Omega = mean_AragSatState
  ) %>%
  filter(
    basin %in% c("atlantic", "pacific"),
    !is.na(Chlorophyll),
    !is.na(Temperature),
    !is.na(Depth),
    !is.na(Omega)
  )

# Define pairwise predictor comparisons
pairwise_collinearity_dat <- bind_rows(
  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Omega vs. Chlorophyll",
      x = Chlorophyll,
      y = Omega
    ),

  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Omega vs. Temperature",
      x = Temperature,
      y = Omega
    ),

  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Omega vs. Depth",
      x = Depth,
      y = Omega
    ),

  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Chlorophyll vs. Temperature",
      x = Temperature,
      y = Chlorophyll
    ),

  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Chlorophyll vs. Depth",
      x = Depth,
      y = Chlorophyll
    ),

  loc2_predictor_dat %>%
    transmute(
      basin, loc1, loc2,
      comparison = "Temperature vs. Depth",
      x = Depth,
      y = Temperature
    )
)

# Correlation table by basin and predictor pair
loc2_collinearity_cor <- pairwise_collinearity_dat %>%
  group_by(basin, comparison) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    correlation = cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs"),
    p.value = cor.test(x, y)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Basin = str_to_title(basin),
    Comparison = comparison
  ) %>%
  select(
    Basin,
    Comparison,
    n,
    correlation,
    p.value
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Correlation = correlation,
    `p value` = p.value
  ) %>%
  arrange(Comparison, Basin)

# Display correlation table
loc2_collinearity_cor %>%
  kbl(
    digits = 3,
    caption = "Pairwise correlations among loc2-level environmental predictors."
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(
    columns = 1,
    valign = "top",
    latex_hline = "major"
  )
```

## Analyze response variables at the loc2 level
```{r lmLoc2ByBasinChlorophyllOmegaDepth_cleanTables, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Define loc2-level BAR response variables in the desired output order
response_vars <- c(
  "meanDeltaMass_mgcm2y",
  "meanDeltaVolume",
  "meanDeltaDensity",
  "meanAccretion",
  "meanGrazing",
  "meanMacroboring"
)

# Response variable display labels with HTML superscripts
response_labels <- c(
  meanDeltaMass_mgcm2y = "Mass (mg cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanDeltaVolume      = "Volume (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanDeltaDensity     = "Density (mg cm<sup>-3</sup>)",
  meanAccretion        = "Accretion (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanGrazing          = "Grazing (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)",
  meanMacroboring      = "Macroboring (mm<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> yr<sup>-1</sup>)"
)

# Define model term order for the full coefficient table
term_order_loc2 <- c(
  "(Intercept)",
  "mean_AragSatState",
  "meanChlorophyll",
  "meanDepth"
)

# Model term display labels
term_labels_loc2 <- c(
  `(Intercept)`       = "Intercept",
  mean_AragSatState   = "Omega",
  meanChlorophyll     = "Chlorophyll",
  meanDepth           = "Depth"
)

# Put the data in long format where "response" is the name of the response variable
# and "value" is the value of that response variable.
model_dat_long <- combinedTableLoc2 %>%
  select(
    basin, loc1, loc2,
    mean_AragSatState,
    meanChlorophyll,
    meanDepth,
    all_of(response_vars)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(response_vars),
    names_to = "response",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    response = factor(response, levels = response_vars)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    basin %in% c("atlantic", "pacific"),
    !is.na(value),
    !is.na(mean_AragSatState),
    !is.na(meanChlorophyll)
  )

# Function to apply basin-specific NA filtering
filter_model_data <- function(dat, loc2ModelBasin) {
  if (loc2ModelBasin == "atlantic") {
    dat %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(mean_AragSatState),
        !is.na(meanChlorophyll)
      )
  } else if (loc2ModelBasin == "pacific") {
    dat %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(mean_AragSatState),
        !is.na(meanChlorophyll),
        !is.na(meanDepth)
      )
  } else {
    stop("Unexpected basin name: ", loc2ModelBasin)
  }
}

# Function to fit basin-specific loc2-level models
# Atlantic: response ~ omega + chlorophyll
# Pacific:  response ~ omega + chlorophyll + depth
fit_loc2_lm <- function(dat, loc2ModelBasin) {
  if (loc2ModelBasin == "atlantic") {
    lm(
      value ~ mean_AragSatState + meanChlorophyll,
      data = dat
    )
  } else if (loc2ModelBasin == "pacific") {
    lm(
      value ~ mean_AragSatState + meanChlorophyll + meanDepth,
      data = dat
    )
  } else {
    stop("Unexpected basin name: ", loc2ModelBasin)
  }
}

# Function to extract model coefficients and model fit statistics
extract_lm_effects <- function(dat, loc2ModelBasin) {
  model_summary <- summary(fit_loc2_lm(dat, loc2ModelBasin))

  as.data.frame(model_summary$coefficients) %>%
    rownames_to_column("term") %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    mutate(
      r_squared = model_summary$r.squared,
      adj_r_squared = model_summary$adj.r.squared
    )
}

# Fit models and create full coefficient table
full_model_effects_loc2 <- model_dat_long %>%
  group_by(basin, response) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model_data = map2(data, basin, filter_model_data),
    n_loc2 = map_int(model_data, nrow),
    n_loc1 = map_int(model_data, ~ n_distinct(.x$loc1)),
    coef_table = map2(model_data, basin, extract_lm_effects)
  ) %>%
  select(
    basin, response,
    n_loc2, n_loc1,
    coef_table
  ) %>%
  unnest(coef_table) %>%
  rename(
    estimate  = Estimate,
    std.error = `Std. Error`,
    statistic = `t value`,
    p.value   = `Pr(>|t|)`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    response = factor(response, levels = response_vars),
    term = factor(term, levels = term_order_loc2)
  ) %>%
  select(
    basin, response,
    n_loc2, n_loc1,
    r_squared, adj_r_squared,
    term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value
  ) %>%
  arrange(response, basin, term) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create display table with formatted labels and capitalization
full_model_effects_loc2_display <- full_model_effects_loc2 %>%
  mutate(
    Response = response_labels[as.character(response)],
    Basin = str_to_title(basin),
    Term = term_labels_loc2[as.character(term)]
  ) %>%
  select(
    Response, Basin,
    n_loc2, n_loc1,
    r_squared, adj_r_squared,
    Term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value
  ) %>%
  rename(
    `N loc2` = n_loc2,
    `N loc1` = n_loc1,
    `R-squared` = r_squared,
    `Adjusted R-squared` = adj_r_squared,
    Estimate = estimate,
    `Std. Error` = std.error,
    `t value` = statistic,
    `p value` = p.value
  )

# Identify significant non-intercept term rows
sig_rows <- which(
  full_model_effects_loc2$`p.value` < 0.05 &
    as.character(full_model_effects_loc2$term) != "(Intercept)"
)

# Full model effects table
full_model_effects_loc2_display %>%
  kbl(
    digits = 4,
    escape = FALSE,
    caption = "Full loc2-level model coefficient table using combinedTableLoc2. Atlantic models include omega and chlorophyll. Pacific models include omega, chlorophyll, and depth."
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE
  ) %>%
  row_spec(
    sig_rows,
    bold = TRUE
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(
    columns = 1,
    valign = "top",
    latex_hline = "major"
  ) %>%
  scroll_box(height = "1500px", width = "100%")
```
